[These two verses must be GargaJs, although they are printed
as those of Varahamihira.]
(11) Portents of Indra's Banner etc.
n©Yti
When Indra's Standard, the cross-bar of a door, pillar, door,
door-leaves, arch or flag, falls down or breaks, it foreshadows the
king's death.
nvaicu
Brilliant light at dawn and sunset, smoke rising from the

390 Brhat Samhita
forest without fire, splitting of the earth without any breach and its
tremor are harmful to the people.
feq: ^ TnTI cT^T kflW ?TT5T: ii^^ll
The country which is ruled by a king, who is devoted to here-
tics and atheists, who has forsaken virtuous conduct, is hot-tempered,
malicious, cruel and always intent on fighting, will be utterly
ruined.
[It is a portent for a country to have a king described in this
verse. By inference we can get Varaha's definition of an ideal
king.
The metre is ynfi-Hf.]
